title: lithocarpus keningauensis
intro: Lithocarpus keningauensis, also known as the Keningau stone oak, is a species of tree in the beech family Fagaceae that is native to the state of Sabah in Malaysia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Lithocarpus keningauensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve extracting its bioactive compounds through methods such as decoction or infusion, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams for various health applications.
